One Truck or Two makes no difference since the trucks are automated and will repeat the same process every time, just in the opposite direction. The main variable in this is the use of the Audi and a human driver. If they had used an EV with a programmed acceleration curve and max speed setting it would have been easy to hit speed target and waypoint early in the run and know with certainty that he would make the jump. I mean seriously, I had this as a physics problem in high school. Except instead of a truck it was a river and you had to swim to the other side of the river to a specific point. You knew the speed of the river and the speed you could swim to determine how far up the river you would need to start swimming to make your target on the other side. I feel like they are waaay to many people that did not take or pass even basic math watching these videos that think this is some magic stuff. The only thing that made these things dangerous in the past is that humans would be driving both trucks making the timing more difficult.
